channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: US envoys will head to Pakistan on Saturday for fresh Iran talks
item.description: Iran's top diplomat is heading to Pakistan for fresh talks, sources say. Live updates: Iran’s top diplomat heads to Pakistan as fresh talks expected, sources say CNN. Live updates: US envoys will head to Pakistan on Saturday for fresh Iran talks CNN.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Trump sending envoys to Pakistan for talks with
item.description: US envoy envoys are heading to Pakistan for talks with Iran's foreign minister, amid a war ceasefire. The US has put its envoy on standby, as the situation remains fragile.

item.title: Trump dispatches Witkoff, Kushner to Pakistan for talks with Iran’s FM Araghchi; Vance on ‘standby’
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is set to arrive in Pakistan tonight for talks with the US, with a US delegation likely to follow on April 25 to discuss a framework for negotiations.

item.title: Kushner and Witkoff to Talk With Iranian Officials in Pakistan
item.description: The Israeli military extended its truce with Lebanon by three weeks, despite striking Hezbollah targets on Friday. The cease-fire aims to end the war in Iran. The Israeli military said it had struck Hezbollah targets on Friday, but its truce with Lebanon appeared to be holding.

item.title: Vance on 'standby' as Witkoff, Kushner to reach Pakistan on April 25 for talks with Iran, White
item.description: Iran's foreign minister is leading a delegation to Islamabad for talks with Pakistani officials. The Iranian delegation will discuss regional developments and the war situation. Iranian delegation led by FM Araghchi to reach Islamabad tonight; to “hold bilateral consultations on developments in the region, as well as the latest situation” in the war.

item.title: US Confirms Talks With Iran, 2 Key Officials To Fly To Pak Tomorrow
item.description: The US has confirmed that two key officials will fly to Pakistan tomorrow for talks with Iran. The talks aim to address the ongoing conflict. US Confirms Talks With Iran, 2 Key Officials To Fly To Pak Tomorrow.

item.title: Netanyahu Accuses Hezbollah of "Trying To Sabotage" Lebanon Peace Efforts
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has accused Hezbollah of trying to sabotage Lebanon's peace efforts. Netanyahu made the statement without providing further details. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu (File). Netanyahu Accuses Hezbollah of "Trying To Sabotage" Lebanon Peace Efforts.

item.title: Three Kosovo Serbs jailed over deadly gun battle and monastery siege
item.description: Kosovo's court has sentenced three Serbs to prison for their roles in a deadly gun battle and monastery siege in 2023. Two men received life imprisonment, while one was sentenced to 30 years.

item.title: Witkoff, Kushner to head to Pakistan for new Iran peace talks, White House says
item.description: The US and Iran remain in a standoff in the Strait of Hormuz, with Hezbollah rejecting a Lebanon ceasefire. Live Updates: U. S. and Iran remain locked in Strait of Hormuz standoff, Hezbollah rejects Lebanon ceasefire CBS News.

item.title: As Putin Orders That the Economy Be Fixed, Russia Grasps for Solutions
item.description: Russia's central bank cut interest rates again amid wartime spending strains. Another interest-rate cut highlights the narrowing path for the country’s central bank amid the strains of immense wartime spending.

item.title: China slaps export curbs on European firms over Taiwan arms sales
item.description: China imposed export curbs on seven European firms over Taiwan arms sales. They prohibit exports of "dual-use items" to the seven named entities or transfers of such items that originated in China by "foreign organisations and individuals", the statement said.

item.title: Russian oil exports slump as Ukraine hammers ports and refineries
item.description: Russian oil exports fell due to Ukraine's attacks on ports and refineries. Despite US sanctions waiver, Russian oil exports could fall to lowest levels since 2023, sources say. Russian oil exports slump as Ukraine hammers ports and refineries.

item.title: U.S. soldier charged with using classified intel to win $400K Polymarket bet on Maduro raid
item.description: US soldier Gannon Van Dyke was charged with using classified intel for betting. Gannon Ken Van Dyke allegedly placed those bets between December 30 and January 2, with the vast majority occurring the night of January 2.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire amid Hormuz uncertainty
item.description: Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire amid uncertainty in the Hormuz region. Live updates: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire amid Hormuz uncertainty NBC News.

item.title: Is the U.S. Trying to Suspend Spain From NATO? Sánchez Addresses Reported Pentagon Email
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Sanchez denied a reported US plan to suspend Spain from NATO, saying the country's position on the Iran war remains firm and unchanged. Is the U. S. Trying to Suspend Spain From NATO?

item.title: 1980s Tanker War History: A Blueprint For Today's US-Iran Conflict
item.description: The US Navy is familiar with Iran's small boat tactics from the 1980s Tanker War. The US Navy has long been familiar with the small boat tactics deployed by Iran's Revolutionary Guard.

item.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks
item.description: Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire by three weeks after US-brokered talks. U. S. President Donald Trump announces the development after meeting representatives from both countries in Washington. Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks.

item.title: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
item.description: The US justice department dropped its probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell. President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building. US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell.

item.title: Woman Trapped In Poo For 3 Hours After Outdoor Toilet Collapses In Australian Outback
item.description: A tourist was rescued after being trapped in a pit toilet for three hours. A tourist trapped in a pit toilet for three hours was rescued safely. Woman Trapped In Poo For 3 Hours After Outdoor Toilet Collapses In Australian Outback.

item.title: US has three aircraft carriers in the Middle East for first time since 2003
item.description: The US has deployed three aircraft carriers in the Middle East for the first time since 2003, as part of a blockade on Iran's ports, amid a fragile truce and escalating tensions.
